name: Greece (Ελλάδα)
summary: Greece is a civil-law unitary republic whose family-court framework operates under the Astikos Kodikas (Civil Code) Book Four (Family Law). Parental responsibility (γονική μέριμνα / goniki merimna) is governed by AK arts. 1510-1541; joint exercise of parental responsibility was strengthened by Law 4800/2021 in force 16 September 2021, which established explicit statutory provisions on shared residence, contact-time presumptions, and contact-obstruction sanctions. The Areios Pagos (Court of Cassation, Athens) is the apex court for civil matters; family-law decisions reach Areios Pagos via the Efeteio (Court of Appeal) following first-instance Monomeles Protodikeio (District Court) determinations. Psychology profession is regulated under Law 991/1979 and the subsequent legal framework; statutory licensing operates under the Ministry of Health. Greece is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, but the Law 4800/2021 explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate engaged the PA-construct as part of its policy basis, making Greece notable within the corpus for explicit legislative reference to alienating-conduct concerns even without statutory PAS codification.

statutory_framework.citation: Astikos Kodikas arts. 1510-1541 (as amended by Law 4800/2021)
statutory_framework.title: Civil Code Book Four — Parental Responsibility
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal civil-code basis for parental responsibility. Law 4800/2021 (in force 16 September 2021) substantially reformed the AK family-law framework: strengthened joint-exercise of parental responsibility; introduced presumption of minimum 1/3 contact-time; established statutory grounds for sanctioning sustained obstruction of contact. Central provisions for any PA-adjacent custody-modification analysis.

statutory_framework.title: Νόμος 4800/2021 — Reform of family-law framework
statutory_framework.relevance: Explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate for Law 4800/2021 explicitly engaged the parental-alienation construct as part of the policy basis for the reform. Makes Greece notable within the corpus for legislative-record engagement with PA reasoning without statutory codification of PAS as a diagnostic label.

statutory_framework.title: Psychologists Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute regulating the psychology profession. Statutory licensing under the Ministry of Health. Statutory anchor for evaluator-quality in court-appointed psychological assessments.

apex_courts.name: Areios Pagos (Court of Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Apex court of cassation for civil and criminal matters. Family-law decisions reach Areios Pagos via the Efeteio (Court of Appeal) following Monomeles Protodikeio (District Court) determinations. Operates as a cassation court reviewing legal-question determinations.

professional_regulators.name: Hellenic Psychological Society (Ελληνική Ψυχολογική Εταιρεία / ELPSE)
professional_regulators.role: Peak academic-and-professional psychology society in Greece. Operates ethics code and complaints procedure.

professional_regulators.name: Panhellenic Psychological Association (Σύλλογος Ελλήνων Ψυχολόγων / SEPS)
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional union of Greek psychologists. Operates ethics code and complaints procedure alongside Ministry-of-Health licensing under Law 991/1979.

anonymisation_convention: Greek family-law decisions are anonymised per Areios Pagos and Efeteio practice using initials. Published decisions on the Areios Pagos portal strip identifying details.

key_developments.title: Law 991/1979 Psychologists Act
key_developments.description: Federal statutory regulation of psychology profession enacted.

key_developments.title: Family Law modernisation (Law 1329/1983)
key_developments.description: Substantial modernisation of Astikos Kodikas Book Four — abolition of patriarchal family-law structure; gender-equal parental responsibility; civil marriage; no-fault divorce.

key_developments.title: Law 4800/2021 in force
key_developments.description: Substantial reform of parental-responsibility framework in force 16 September 2021. Strengthened joint-exercise default; introduced presumption of minimum 1/3 contact-time; established statutory grounds for sanctioning sustained contact-obstruction. Legislative-record engagement with PA-construct reasoning in the explanatory memorandum and parliamentary debate.

structural_findings: Greece sits structurally within the EU civil-law cluster — Astikos Kodikas + Law 4800/2021 reform + Areios Pagos cassation apex. Law 4800/2021 makes Greece notable for explicit legislative-record engagement with PA-construct reasoning even without statutory PAS codification — structurally distinct from Brazil (Lei 12.318/2010) and Mexico (state codes) which codified PA-related provisions explicitly.

Law 4800/2021 contact-time presumption (minimum 1/3) and obstruction-sanction framework places Greece structurally adjacent to Belgium's astreintes regime (Civil Code art. 387ter) and to the Brazilian Lei 12.318/2010 framework — without naming PA in statutory text.

Psychology profession regulation operates under Law 991/1979 + Ministry of Health licensing — less centralised than the federal-statutory regimes elsewhere in the corpus but still operates statutory title oversight.
editorial_notes: Greece jurisdiction sidecar — EU civil-law framework. AK + Law 4800/2021 reform + Areios Pagos cassation + Law 991/1979 psychology regulation.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — but Law 4800/2021 explanatory memorandum explicitly engaged PA-construct reasoning. Structurally distinct from Brazil/Mexico explicit codification.
Joins EU civil-law cluster + Iberian/Mediterranean sub-cluster within the corpus.
